frontier: Can ex(Q7,C4) exceed 305 edges? The current lower bound is 304 (arXiv:2603.29127, two-phase simulated annealing); the exact value remains open.
action:
Milestone: bounded_exact_search; contract=sha256:5c3f895292d59b11fc8f37a0f70c223f24ee5aa5a6d73b5948bfd5c1e9c4c69b
Replayed the prior-session q7_c4_verifier.py (kill-fixture pass), then attempted a bounded symmetry-aware local search seeded from a greedy C4-breaking initial condition targeting ≥305 edges. The search timed out (60s clamp) because the greedy C4-breaking loop was too slow in pure Python without incremental square-count tracking.
verified:
Verifier domain: edge sets that are subsets of Q7's 448 edges.
Verifier predicate: no 4-cycle (square) of Q7 is fully contained in the edge set.
Domain kill fixtures: 3/3 rejected correctly
Predicate kill fixtures: 2/2 rejected correctly
ALL KILL FIXTURES PASSED
Artifact: q7_c4_verifier.py, SHA-256: bbbcd39be07e7c06beb14a88cec46189ddd9a577b3bfadf7686800ed3177b3f3
result: Negative result / resource blocker. The verifier replay is solid and passes. The bounded local search failed to execute within the 30-second terminal action budget due to pure-Python performance in the greedy C4-breaking phase (O(squares × edges) per step without incremental bookkeeping). No candidate ≥305-edge construction was produced. The route is not mathematically closed — the search method needs incremental square-count tracking (per-edge: set of squares containing that edge, incrementally updated on add/remove) to complete within budget.
next_gate: Rewrite the local search with incremental square-count tracking (maintain for each edge the count of unsatisfied squares it belongs to; update counts in O(degree_in_square_graph) per add/remove) and rerun the ≤30s bounded search seeded from the arXiv:2603.29127 304-edge construction. If the incremental version still cannot beat 304, report negative-result closure for this search method.
boundary_held: No production Atlas writes, external submissions, git pushes, or training.
